runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "python", "build_system": "pip", "package_manager": "pip", "build_commands": ["pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt"], "install_commands": ["apt-get update -y", "apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends libgl1-mesa-glx libx11-6 libxext6 libxrender1 tk", "rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "3.11", "system_packages": ["libgl1-mesa-glx", "libx11-6", "libxext6", "libxrender1", "tk"]}, "repo_type": "desktop_app", "base_image": "python:3.11.15-bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Official Python image with Python 3.11 on Debian bookworm provides a compatible, widely-supported base for installing the project's Python dependencies.", "pitfalls": ["The requirements.txt pulls in GPU-specific onnxruntime packages conditionally; in a CPU-only Docker build these wheels may be skipped or cause conflicts if incompatible with the host GPU drivers.", "GUI inside a container requires an X server or VNC; running run.py may fail without display forwarding. The project expects tkinter GUI."], "notes": "The container will install Python dependencies from requirements.txt, which include heavy ML libraries. Consider using a non-interactive mode in Docker and disable GUI-related components if you only need CLI."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
FROM python:3.11.15-bookworm

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required for GUI and OpenCV
RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        libgl1-mesa-glx \
        libx11-6 \
        libxext6 \
        libxrender1 \
        tk \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create a non-root user for security
RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -m -r -g appuser -d /home/appuser -s /bin/bash appuser

# Prepare working directory and set ownership to non-root user
WORKDIR /app
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser requirements.txt ./
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .

# Ensure the app directory is owned by the non-root user
RUN chown -R appuser:appuser /app

USER appuser

CMD ["python","run.py"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM python:3.11.15-bookworm

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        libgl1-mesa-glx \
        libx11-6 \
        libxext6 \
        libxrender1 \
        tk \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Install Python dependencies first (cached layer)
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY . .

# Default command to run the application (GUI)
CMD ["python","run.py"]
"}

{"path": ".dockerignore", "content": "# Byte-size optimization and to avoid building context from local environment
.git
node_modules
__pycache__
*.pyc
*.pyo
*.pyd
venv
*.egg-info
.DS_Store
media
models
"}

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies required for GUI and OpenCV
RUN apt-get update -y \

        libxext6 \
        libxrender1 \
        tk \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Create a non-root user for security
RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -m -r -g appuser -d /home/appuser -s /bin/bash appuser

# Prepare working directory and set ownership to non-root user
WORKDIR /app
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser requirements.txt ./
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy the rest of the application code
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .

# Ensure the app directory is owned by the non-root user

USER appuser

CMD ["python","run.py"]
